channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Old muscle stem cells can act young again but there is a catch
item.description: UCLA scientists found that a protein called NDRG1 accumulates in older muscle stem cells, slowing healing.

item.title: Scientists make quantum time flow backward in stunning physics breakthrough
item.description: Researchers developed quantum control techniques that can make a system appear to run backward in time.

item.title: Bumble bees surprise scientists by solving a classic intelligence test
item.description: Bumble bees amazed researchers by inventing a new method to access a hidden reward without prior training. This finding adds to growing evidence of insect intelligence.

item.title: Unusual LIGO signal may point to the missing link behind dark matter
item.description: A peculiar gravitational wave signal has revived hopes that primordial black holes, once thought purely theoretical, could soon be detected.
